MORY ACCESS ARCHITECTURE by S. Sriram and E. A. Lee Proceedings of IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing Minneapolis, MN, Vol. I pp. 345-348, April 1993 ABSTRACT This paper describes a multiprocessor machine for r

his paper describes a multiprocessor machine for real-time Digital Signal Processing that uses commercial programmable DSP chips. The architecture is a shared memory, single shared bus parallel processor designed to run signal processing tasks that c

sor designed to run signal processing tasks that can be statically scheduled.The design is based on the architecture proposed in [1]. A prototype has since been built. The implementation details and performance results are discussed here. 1. This wor
erformance results are discussed here. 1. This work was supported by grants from SRC (grant number 92-DC-008), Motorola Inc., and ArielCorp.
